title: Harakiri
tmdbOverview: Down-on-his-luck veteran Tsugumo Hanshirō enters the courtyard of the prosperous House of Iyi. Unemployed, and with no family, he hopes to find a place to commit seppuku—and a worthy second to deliver the coup de grâce in his suicide ritual. The senior counselor for the Iyi clan questions the ronin’s resolve and integrity, suspecting Hanshirō of seeking charity rather than an honorable end. What follows is a pair of interlocking stories which lay bare the difference between honor and respect, and promises to examine the legendary foundations of the Samurai code.
synopsis: Released in 1962, Harakiri is a action, drama and history film from Japan. It's highly rated, holding a strong 8.6 on IMDb. A standout of 1960s cinema, it earns its spot on our lists. It carries a 18 certification (DE rating system) — see the film's page for parental details.
